headlines.title: Israel denying Palestinian women medical care in prison
headlines.snippet: A report by the Palestinian Prisoners and Ex-Prisoners Affairs Commission states that Palestinian women in Israel’s Damon Prison are experiencing "catastrophic" conditions, including overcrowding, medical neglect, and denial of basic human rights. Pregnant prisoners are reportedly denied necessary medical care and basic necessities.

headlines.title: UN report: 80 percent of Gaza living in dire conditions
headlines.snippet: According to the UN's Office for the Coordination of Humanitarian Affairs, approximately 80 percent of Gaza's population, nearly 1.7 million displaced Palestinians, face severe shortages of water, shelter, and basic services. Thousands are housed in overcrowded shelters or sleep outdoors.
